req.title: Transaction dispute (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall dispute a transaction within 60 days of posting; disputes beyond 60 days shall be rejected. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-034 governs the 60-day dispute window with a strict boundary. BVA dictates tests at day 1 (deep interior positive), day 59 (upper interior positive), day 60 (exact boundary — last valid day), day 61 (first invalid day — negative boundary), and a deep negative (e.g., day 90). EP partitions the domain into two equivalence classes: [0,60] (accepted) and (60,∞) (rejected). STC captures the transaction state machine: POSTED → DISPUTED → PROVISIONAL_CREDIT_APPLIED. A DT test validates the combined rule: authenticated customer + valid window → 201 + provisional credit. NEG covers the explicit error pathway (422) for out-of-window disputes. Five tests are included to achieve robust boundary and partition coverage while remaining concise and idempotent.

tests.name: test_dispute_within_window_positive_ep
preconditions: Transaction is in POSTED state and not previously disputed
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-034: disputes within 60 days shall be accepted
R-FIN-034: provisional credit shall be applied on dispute creation
tests.executable:
def test_dispute_within_window_positive_ep(api_client, make_transaction):
    txn = make_transaction(age_days=30)
    payload = {
        "transaction_id": txn["id"],
        "reason": "Unrecognized charge"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/disputes", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "dispute_id" in body
    assert body.get("provisional_credit") is True
    assert body.get("status") == "OPEN"

tests.name: test_dispute_beyond_window_negative_ep
preconditions: Transaction posted 90 days ago (deep inside invalid partition (60,∞))
Transaction is in POSTED state and not previously disputed
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains error code DISPUTE_WINDOW_EXPIRED
trace: R-FIN-034: disputes beyond 60 days shall be rejected with 422
tests.executable:
def test_dispute_beyond_window_negative_ep(api_client, make_transaction):
    txn = make_transaction(age_days=90)
    payload = {
        "transaction_id": txn["id"],
        "reason": "Unrecognized charge"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/disputes", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "DISPUTE_WINDOW_EXPIRED"
    assert "dispute_id" not in body

tests.name: test_dispute_on_day_60_boundary_accepted
preconditions: Transaction posted exactly 60 days ago (upper inclusive boundary)
Transaction is in POSTED state and not previously disputed
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-034: boundary behaviour at exact max threshold (day 60) must be tested
R-FIN-034: day 60 is within the valid window — dispute shall be accepted
tests.executable:
def test_dispute_on_day_60_boundary_accepted(api_client, make_transaction):
    txn = make_transaction(age_days=60)
    payload = {
        "transaction_id": txn["id"],
        "reason": "Duplicate transaction"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/disputes", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "dispute_id" in body
    assert body.get("provisional_credit") is True
    assert body.get("status") == "OPEN"

tests.name: test_dispute_on_day_61_boundary_rejected
preconditions: Transaction posted exactly 61 days ago (first day outside valid window)
Transaction is in POSTED state and not previously disputed
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains error code DISPUTE_WINDOW_EXPIRED
trace: R-FIN-034: boundary behaviour at exact max threshold must be tested
R-FIN-034: day 61 is the first invalid day — dispute shall be rejected
tests.executable:
def test_dispute_on_day_61_boundary_rejected(api_client, make_transaction):
    txn = make_transaction(age_days=61)
    payload = {
        "transaction_id": txn["id"],
        "reason": "Duplicate transaction"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/disputes", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "DISPUTE_WINDOW_EXPIRED"
    assert "dispute_id" not in body

tests.name: test_dispute_state_transition_posted_to_disputed
preconditions: Transaction posted 10 days ago in POSTED state
Transaction has not been disputed previously
actions: Assert transaction initial state is POSTED via GET /transactions/{id}
expected: Transaction state transitions from POSTED to DISPUTED
provisional_credit field is true in dispute record
trace: R-FIN-034: filing a dispute triggers state transition POSTED → DISPUTED
R-FIN-034: provisional credit applied upon successful dispute creation
tests.executable:
def test_dispute_state_transition_posted_to_disputed(api_client, make_transaction):
    txn = make_transaction(age_days=10)
    pre = api_client.get(f"/transactions/{txn['id']}")
    assert pre.status_code == 200
    assert pre.json().get("state") == "POSTED"

    payload = {"transaction_id": txn["id"], "reason": "Merchant error"}
    resp = api_client.post("/disputes", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    dispute_id = body["dispute_id"]
    assert body.get("provisional_credit") is True

    post_txn = api_client.get(f"/transactions/{txn['id']}")
    assert post_txn.json().get("state") == "DISPUTED"

    dispute = api_client.get(f"/disputes/{dispute_id}")
    assert dispute.status_code == 200
    assert dispute.json().get("status") == "OPEN"
